Select what to print
WP sources
Search cards

IPA is case-sensitive. Short queries (1–2 chars) only match short symbols — type more characters to search names, descriptions and category cards.

About & Sources

This app was fully generated by Claude.ai (Claude Sonnet 4.6) — . Content, card data, rules, and all code were produced through a conversation with Claude.

About this app

An interactive flashcard and word list tool for learning the phoneme inventory of Received Pronunciation (RP) — the reference accent of British English.

Cards cover all RP vowels, consonants, syllabic sonorants, suprasegmentals, and key morphophonological rules (-s, -ed endings, silent letters). The word list quiz uses the pronunciation word list from the textbook's appendix.

Phonetic descriptions

Each phoneme card's description (e.g. Short near-close near-front unrounded vowel.) is the official IPA name taken from the relevant Wikipedia article for that phoneme, with "short" or "long" prepended where applicable. These names follow the IPA convention of describing vowels by height, backness and rounding, and consonants by voicing, place and manner.

Where a second sentence is present (e.g. Aspirated word-initially in RP.) it is a Claude.ai addition specific to RP and not from Wikipedia.

Example word transcriptions

The word list is based on the pronunciation word list from the textbook's appendix. Additional words from card rules and exceptions have been added. Words whose IPA was generated by Claude — rather than taken directly from the book — are highlighted in blue in the word list so you can review and correct them.

Source marker legend

Small superscript markers on card descriptions, rules, and mnemonic pills indicate the source. Hover over a sentence to reveal its marker. WP markers are clickable links.

WP
Wikipedia — text or terminology taken from a Wikipedia article, licensed under CC BY-SA 4.0. Click the marker to open the source article.
GPF
General phonological fact — a standard phonological grouping not specific to this textbook but uncontroversial in the field.
CAI
Claude.ai editorial — a pedagogically useful addition by Claude.ai, not directly traceable to any source. Treat with appropriate scepticism.
user
User-edited — a mnemonic you wrote or modified yourself. Appears on the mnemonic pill whenever you have overridden the default suggestion.
Credits

IPA chart — International Phonetic Alphabet in IPA Kiel, IPA_Kiel.pdf, available under a Creative Commons Attribution-Sharealike 4.0 International licence. Copyright © 2026 International Phonetic Association. Accessed 14 March 2026.

Audio — Phoneme audio samples from Wikipedia · Help:IPA, hosted by Wikimedia Commons. Licenced under CC BY-SA 3.0. Individual file attributions available on each file's Wikimedia Commons page.

Wikipedia — Phoneme descriptions use material from Wikipedia articles for individual IPA sounds (e.g. Near-close near-front unrounded vowel), released under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International License. Source articles are linked via the WP markers on individual card sentences.

Speech synthesis — Powered by Google Cloud Text-to-Speech. Use of this service is subject to the Google Cloud Platform Terms of Service. This app uses Google TTS for personal, non-commercial study only. Generated audio is not stored or redistributed.

Pronunciation assessment — Powered by Microsoft Azure AI Speech (Pronunciation Assessment). Use of this service is subject to the Microsoft Azure Terms of Service and the Microsoft AI Code of Conduct. Audio recordings are sent to Microsoft Azure (North Europe) for processing and are not stored by this app. This feature is used for personal study only.

Fonts — Noto Serif by Production Type and Source Code Pro by Colophon Foundry, served via Google Fonts. Both licensed under the SIL Open Font License 1.1.

Feedback

Tested on Apple iOS and Windows / Firefox only. Bug reports and feature requests are welcome at .

🎉

Ah, you found the edit mode — nice! This app is free, and considerable time and effort has gone into creating it.

If you come up with a great mnemonic, consider donating it by emailing it to — your contribution might help other learners!

Mnemonics

Memory hooks shown on card backs. Claude.ai derived — feel free to be creative! If you found a gem, send it to .

Notes

Notes appear on every card back. All edits are saved to your browser.

Paste your exported JSON below and click Import.

Copy this JSON and save it as a .json file. Use Import to restore it in a new version.

⚠ ADVANCED USERS ONLY

Clearing entries will permanently delete your saved data for that key. This cannot be undone. Only clear an entry if you know what it contains and why you want to remove it.

Edit note
Reference Resources
IPA chart Official IPA chart — consonants, vowels, diacritics, suprasegmentals ↗ IPA
Tongue positions of Daniel Jones' cardinal front vowels. Four large dots = highest tongue point per vowel. Wikimedia Commons · CC BY-SA 3.0
Edit IPA
Speech
Text to Speech Google Cloud / Web Speech
Provider
About Google TTS

Uses the Google Cloud Text-to-Speech API with natural British English (Wavenet) voices. Audio is generated on demand and not stored.

Free tier: 4 million characters/month for Wavenet voices — far more than needed for personal study use. Requires a Google Cloud account with billing enabled (credit card required, but you won't be charged within the free tier).

Your API key is stored only in your browser's local storage and sent only to Google's API.

Creating a Google Cloud account and obtaining an API key is beyond the scope of this app — Google and AI are your friends for that. Start here: cloud.google.com/text-to-speech.

→ Google TTS pricing page  ·  → Enable API & get key

API Key
⚠ Monthly limit reached (2M characters). Speech is disabled until next month.
Local counter: 0 / 2,000,000 chars ↗ Cloud Console
Pronunciation method

When enabled, Google TTS receives the IPA transcription via SSML <phoneme>. Only Studio voices reliably support this. When disabled, plain text is sent and Google uses its own pronunciation model. Note: transcriptions in this app use the conventional phonemic symbol r, but Google's en-GB phoneme table requires ɹ (the strictly precise IPA symbol for the English approximant). The substitution is applied automatically before sending — your data is not affected.

About Web Speech

Uses the browser's built-in speech synthesis — completely free, no API key needed. Voice quality depends on your OS and installed voices.

Downloading other voices:

Note: all Web Speech voices use the same underlying speech synthesiser built into your OS or browser — switching voices changes the vocal character, not the synthesis engine. They will not produce more accurate IPA-based pronunciation than the default voice.

Windows 11: Settings → Time & Language → Speech → Manage voices
macOS: System Settings → Accessibility → Spoken Content → System Voice → Manage Voices
iOS: Settings → Accessibility → Spoken Content → Voices → English → English (UK) — download Enhanced or Premium for best quality

⚠ On iOS, Web Speech may be unreliable when opening this file locally (not via a web server). If voices don't load or audio cuts out, try Google TTS instead.

⚠ No en-GB voices found — using OS default.
Voice
How to use

Each word row has a 🔊 button that plays its pronunciation. The 🔊 in the table header plays all words in the current letter group in sequence.

To play from a specific word: right-click (desktop) or long-press (touch) the 🔊 button and choose Play from here. The same gesture on the 🎤 button offers Assess from here.

Play all delay
0s
Disclaimer

Pronunciation is generated by a speech model and is not guaranteed to match the IPA transcriptions in this app or standard British RP. Quality and accuracy depend on the speech model used. Use as a general guide only.

Pronunciation Assessment Microsoft Azure AI Speech
Pronunciation Assessment

Record yourself saying a word — each phoneme is scored against RP. The 🎤 button works three ways simultaneously: hold to record (release stops), tap once to start / tap again to stop, or simply stay silent for 1 second to auto-stop. A correct score automatically advances to the next word.

To assess from a specific word: right-click (desktop) or long-press (touch) the 🎤 button and choose Assess from here. Each word auto-records and advances in sequence.

Requires a free Azure account. Free tier: 5 hours/month (~18,000 seconds). At roughly 3 seconds per word, that's around 6,000 assessments/month — no charge within the free tier.

Region: North Europe (fixed). Your key is stored only in your browser and sent only to Azure.

Creating an Azure account and Speech resource is beyond the scope of this app — AI assistants are your friends for that. Start here: signup.azure.com/signup.

→ Create Azure Speech resource

⚠ Privacy notice — When you use pronunciation assessment, your voice recordings are sent to and processed by Microsoft Azure. Do not use this feature if you are not comfortable with your voice being uploaded to a third-party cloud service.
Score threshold — "correct"
90
Usage: 0 s / 18,000 s
How scores work — after recording, the IPA transcription is coloured per phoneme: green (≥ threshold), amber (60–threshold), red (<60). The badge shows the word-level accuracy score from Azure, which reflects overall fluency and may differ from individual phoneme scores — a word can have all-green phonemes but a lower word score due to prosody or stress timing.
⚠ Accuracy disclaimer — Pronunciation scoring is performed by an AI model trained primarily on American and general British English. It assesses deviation from a statistical average, not strict RP. Phoneme-level scores for en-GB are provided without phoneme symbol labels (an Azure limitation). Scores should be treated as rough guidance only — a low score does not necessarily mean your pronunciation is wrong, and a high score does not guarantee it is correct. Use in combination with listening to reference audio, not as a substitute for a qualified teacher.
